relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Grief over a failed leader is given a boundary but not mocked.
Samuel names the lethal danger of succession.
Calling moves through guarded action rather than careless exposure.
Bethlehem trembles when religious authority arrives.
Peace must be spoken before ritual begins.
Samuel first mistakes appearance for destiny.
The Divine gaze interrupts the old fascination with height.
One impressive son passes.
Another passes.
Seven visible possibilities are refused.
The working youngest son must be summoned from the sheep.
David arrives with beauty still named, even after appearance was questioned.
Oil is poured among brothers, and Spirit moves upon him.
Saul experiences terrifying inner disturbance as Spirit-language shifts around him.
Servants recognize suffering before the king names it.
They propose skilled music as care.
Saul accepts help and asks for competence.
A courtier describes David through an almost impossible bundle of gifts.
The shepherd is summoned into royal space.
His family sends food and an animal with him.
Saul loves David and places him near military power.
The court asks to retain the young worker.
Music brings real relief, though no single practice can carry every form of suffering.

contemplative_note: The chapter holds two truths together: leadership may pass from Saul, and Saul's suffering still deserves compassionate care. Spiritual language must never become stigma. David's music is healing presence, not proof that distress is demonic or that art replaces medicine, safety, consent, and ongoing support.
